concept: Dog / The Companion at the Side
description: The dog: humanity's oldest companion animal, the one who chose us back. Coined by owner ruling — a language of community was incomplete without the species that invented interspecies community. Friendly, callable, at your side: 'hupo'.
sound_symbolism: 'hu' is the happy pant, 'po' the presence at your side — two syllables you can call across a field, which is the practical test every dog-word in every language has passed.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'hupo nua mia thalo.' (The dog walks with me.) 'hupo lo lopia thema.' (The dog guards the children.) Vocatively, like a name: 'kona hupo.' (Here, dog.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The dog is proof that trust can cross a species line and hold for ten thousand years — companionship as practiced, wordless nonviolence.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Herding, guarding, hauling, warming: the dog was the first coworker, paid in belonging — the oldest contract never written.
